titleStmt.title: Chicago, Art Institute of Chicago, AIC 2002.4
Taʾammǝra Māryām

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: Introductory text
Sǝbḥata Dǝngǝl
Yǝweddǝsǝwwa malāʾǝkt la-Māryām
Malkǝʾ-hymn to Our Lady Mary
Malkǝʾ-hymn to Our Lady Mary
Hymn to Our Lady Mary
Hymn to Our Lady Mary
ʾƎsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki ʾǝsaggǝd laki wa-ʾǝweddǝsaki
Taʾammǝra Māryām
miracles
Miracle of Mary: The conception and nativity of St Mary
Miracle of Mary: St Mary stays in the Temple
Miracle of Mary: St Mary is espoused to St Joseph
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, First Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 1: Introduction
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1108MMDaqseyos)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 2, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1109MMWorker)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 3, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1110MMJew)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 4, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1111MMDamianus)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Bishop of Rome, ʾAbbās (Leo)
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 5, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1112MMAbbas)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the monk Isaac
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 6, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1113MMYeshaq)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the young girl called Mary, from Dafrā
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 7, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1114MMMaryam)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a French painter whose scaffold was overthrown by the devil
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 8, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1115MMFrenchman)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of a sick man in a hospital whom Mary carries to Jerusalem
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 9, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1116MMJerusalem)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the youth Zechariah, from Rome
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 10, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1117MMZakaryas)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Juliana and Barbara from Bethlehem
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 11, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1118MMYolyana)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of three Arabs in Rīf and Salomon, abbot of Kalamon
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 12, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1119MMArabs)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the construction of a church in Jericho
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 13, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1120MMSea)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the blind priest John from the church of St Mercurius in Cairo
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 14, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1121MMYohannes)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 15, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1122MMGreek)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Abraham, his wife Gerā and their blind daughter Elisabeth from Badramān
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 16, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1123MMElsabet)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a widow and her poor daughters Maria, Martha, and Yawāhit
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 17, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1124MMWidow)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the two scribes who were brothers
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 18, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1125MMScribes)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of George the Younger, who was delivered from prison
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 19, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1126MMGiyorgis)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the old priest Qaṭīr at ʾƎlkǝsus (al-Khusus)
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 20, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1127MMQatir)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the two brothers Tāg and Nazīb from Dalgā
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 21, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1128MMDyers)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a European man with a petrified foot
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 22, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1129MMFrank)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the leprous Bishop Mercurius

Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a woman from Hartalom/Hartarom with broken legs

Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel

Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bəʾsi collection, Miracle
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Phoenician evil man called Bārok

Miracle of Mary: The Story of the Cannibal of Qǝmr

supportDesc.foliation: There is foliation provided in the bottom of each folio

t:list.item: 3 Fols 156r–158vs.l. 1, no stub (?) The structure of this quire is not discernible because of the quality of the images.

layout.ab: Primary pricks are visible, horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

handNote.t:seg: The name St Mary (and other holy names), the first two lines of the explicit of each narrations, the first lines of each stanza, and all the captions of the miniatures are rubricated.

decoDesc.summary: The manuscript is richly decorated with 74 miniatures that belong to the so-called First Gondarine style of decoration. Paintings in the manuscript are mainly of two types on the basis of their appearance: the first miniatures are introductory images related to the Virgin St Mary and other images related to the life of Christ and other Saints. The second and the most central miniatures in the manuscript are those that correspond to the thirty-two narrations.

decoNote.t:desc: A miniature of the art theme Joachim and Anne grieving for not having a child with two episodes, showing: first Joachim and Anne feeling sad for not having a child, and second, an announcing to them that their prayer is received by God and telling them they will have a daughter soon.

A miniature of the art theme Presentation of Mary with two episodes, showing: first Joachim and Anne, the later carrying a child, both going to the temple, and second, the child St Mary being blessed by two priests in the temple.
A miniature of the art theme Mary fed by an Angel with one episode showing from the infancy of Mary in which she is fed in the Temple by an .

A miniature of the art theme Annunciation with two episodes showing: first, the , named Gabriel, in the aspect of an old man, informing the Virgin St Mary, who is portrayed in the act of spinning, that she will become the mother of Jesus.
A miniature of the art theme Visitation with one episode showing St Mary saluting ʾElsābeṭ, surrounded by other relatives.

A miniature of the art theme Nativity of Jesus with episodes, showing: first St Mary sitting near to the child Jesus Christ who is lying on a garment manger, and second, a cow and donkey breathing on the child Jesus Christ giving Him heat with Joseph, Salome and other three angels surrounded in the depiction.
A miniature of the art themeMassacre of the Innocents with one episode showing the infanticide ordered by Herod, sitting on a throne and his soldiers slaughtering the children.

A miniature of the art theme Flight into Egypt with one episode showing, St Mary who is carrying her child Jesus Christ mounted on donkey and Joseph and Salome standing before and after them respectively.

A miniature of the art theme Temptation of Jesus with two episodes, showing: first, Satan in the aspect of an elderly man appearing to Jesus Christ and trying to tempt him, and second, Satan with his real demonized image flying far from Jesus Christ who is performing a gesture of blessing.

A miniature of the art theme Baptism of Jesus with two episodes, showing: first, Jesus Christ standing and praying near to the river Jordan, and second, St John the Baptist baptizing Jesus Christ in the river Jordan. The is in the form of a dove descending from above.

A miniature with two episodes, showing: first, Saint St Mary who is reaching her two hands praying at Golgotha over a tomb which is surrounded by keepers; and second, the apparition of Our Lord Jesus Christ to St Mary Mary.
A miniature with one episode showing a group of angels and prophets surrounding St Mary bowing for the glorification of the Virgin Saint St Mary who is standing with her hands crossed on her chest.
A miniature with one episode showing Jesus Christ providing hopes to his mother St Mary who is sitting near to the him, surrounded by angels, saints, prophets and David (playing his baganā).
A miniature with one episode where our Lord Jesus Christ is showing hell to St Mary and the depiction is surrounded by angels.
A miniature showing Saint St Mary in Qarānǝyo with two episodes: first St Mary grieving about sinners and second St Mary who is standing with stretched hands praying about the sinners.
A miniature of the art theme Covenant of Mercy with one episode where Our Lord Jesus Christ and His mother Saint St Mary standing in the center of the scene and surrounded by angels.
A miniature of the art theme Dormition of Mary with one episode where the Virgin St Mary is depicted asleep in the center of the scene and surrounded by apostles with some handling lights.
A miniature of the art theme Assumption of Mary with one episode where St Mary is standing with her hands crossed on her chest, surrounded by angels on clouds.

A miniature with one episode showing a group of people with traditional Ethiopian musical instruments surrounding and playing music for the glorification of the Virgin Saint St Mary who is sitting in the center of the depiction.
The iconography of the art theme Virgin and Child showing the icon Virgin and Child and the angles Michael and Gabriel depicted left and right respectively.
A miniature with one episode, showing the Lord Jesus Christ depicted with His mother St Mary and teaching the twelve Apostles Portrait who are surrounding the scene.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Daqsǝyos with four episodes, showing: first Hildephonsus of Toledo who is sitting on a throne and giving the book of the Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo to St Mary who is sitting near to him on a throne; second, Hildephonsus of Toledo receiving a gift of vestment and throne from St Mary; third, Hildephonsus of Toledo's successor who is sitting on a throne reads the Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo and fourth, the successor bishop falling and being smitten by Raguel for having touched St Mary's gifts to Hildephonsus of Toledo without permission.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Mary speaks to a worker with two episodes, showing: first, the peasant standing and praying near to the icon Virgin and Child and second, St Mary who is carrying her Child, in the same depiction blessing the peasant.

A miniature illustrating Miracle of Mary: The Jew of ʾAkmim with four episodes, showing: first, the Jewish priest with crossed hands praying in front of St Mary who is sitting on a throne with the two deacons in the background; second, the Jewish priest lying on the ground after his fall from excess bowing and third, St Mary raising the Jewish priest by her hand.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Dǝmyanos who wrote Mary's name in golden ink with of two episodes, showing: first, Dǝmyānos writing the name of St Mary in a manuscript and second, Dǝmyānos lying down before the icon of Mary, performing a gesture of blessing (in the background, the pillar of gold, on which St Mary inscribed Dǝmyānos' name is depicted).

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: ʾAbbās, bishop of Rome with three episodes, showing: first, the bishop ʾAbbās standing and distributing holy communion surrounded by the faithful, among whom is the woman who kisses his hand; second, the bishop sitting and cutting off his hand, and third, the bishop and the faithful standing in front of the icon of the Virgin and Child, which has healed his hand.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The monk, Yǝsḥaq, who prayed for seven years that St Mary would appear to him with two episodes showing: first, the monk Yǝsḥaq falling on the ground during the apparition of Mary, and second, the icon of St Mary taking Yǝsḥaq by the hand and raising him.
A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Māryām of Dafrā with one episode, showing: St Mary standing next to Māryām of Dafrā where the house represented with Jerusalem is depicted near to her.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The French artist who fell from the scaffolding while painting frescoes with three episodes, showing: first, the painter sitting on a scaffold and drawing a scene; second, the painter falling down from the scaffold and third, the icon of St Mary extending her hand to save the painter.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Mary carries a sick man to Jerusalem with four episodes, showing, first, two men carrying the sick pilgrim on bed; second the sick pilgrim standing before the places to which St Mary carries him; third, Golgotha, Jerusalem, the mount of Olives, Bethlehem and the Jordan are depicted; and fourth, St Mary is standing in the middle of the places depicted with a house scene background.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Zakāryās, who used to bring roses to the icon of St Mary with five episodes, showing: first, Zakārǝyās praying the greeting of the angel before the icon of Virgin and Child; second, Zakārǝyās nailing down in front of the icon of Virgin and Child; third, Zakārǝyās decorating Saint Mary's crown in the icon of Virgin and Child with roses; fourth, St Mary who is sitting on a throne surrounded by angels and the five virgins (carrying roses) and collects roses out of his mouth and fifth, the thieves being consecrated as monks.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Yolyānā and Barbārā from Bethlehem with two episodes, showing: first, the two thieves attempting to eat the bread they stole from Yolyānā and Barbārā and second, the two thieves standing before an icon of St Mary being consecrated as monks.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The three Arabs journeying to Rif and Salomon, the abbot of Qalamon with six episodes, showing: first, the three Arabs swimming in the water; second, one man being eaten by a crocodile; third, the two others standing on a rock above the water; fourth, one man riding a camel to the monastery, which is depicted in the background; fifth, the other man bringing his sheep's wool and sixth, the camel brought by the Arab eating from a tree.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The church of ʿƎqonā, which was removed to the edge of the sea with three episodes, showing: first, St Mary surrounded by the sleeping monks of the monastery, which is depicted in the background; second, the monastery with its monks that has been moved to the shore of the see and third, a group of people marvelling at the miracle.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The blind priest, Yoḥannǝs Bakansi with two episodes, showing: first, the priest Yoḥannǝs is depicted standing and praying before the icon of the Virgin and Child and second, St Mary taking out her breast and dropping her milk into the eyes of Yoḥannǝs.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow with four episodes, showing: first, the rich man from Qʷalāsis travelling with his companions on a ship; second, three individuals from Qʷǝz piercing the eye of the nobleman; third, the rich man fallen after his eye has been pierced by arrows from the enemies and fourth, the rich man standing next to a small statue of himself in front of the icon of the Virgin and Child.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Elizabeth, the blind daughter of Abraham and Gerā ʾAnǝśt of Badǝrmān with four episodes, showing: first, the woman and her blind daughter standing and praying before the icon of the Virgin and Child ; second, both lying asleep before the icon of Virgin and Child; third St Mary waking up the girl, and fourth, St Mary giving sight to the blind girl by dropping her breast's milk.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The widow who could not find husbands for her three daughters with three episodes, showing: first, the poor widow praying before the icon of the Virgin and Child where her three daughters are sitting below her; second, the funeral of the eldest daughter, who is wrapped in a shroud and surrounded by persons, and third, the two other daughters standing next to the arborist man called Tewos.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Giyorgis Ḥaddis, who was delivered from prison with two episodes, showing: first, Giyorgis lying in prison bound at his hands and feet, with a dove appearing to him; second, Giyorgis standing healed from his wounds.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Kaṭir, the old priest at ʾƎlkǝsus with three episodes, showing: first, the custodian of the church standing in front of the sitting Kaṭir ordering him to leave the church; second, St Mary sitting on a throne surrounded by two angels with the custodian of the church sitting before her; third, the healed Kaṭir standing before St Mary, who is sitting on throne.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The two brothers Tāg and Nazib from Dalgā who were dyers with seven episodes, showing: first, the two brothers and the Muslim customer gathered around a piece of cloth; second, the Muslim holding the cloth of Nazib with an interest to kiss him for preparing him cloths; third, the Muslim denouncing Nazib to the judge; fourth, Nazib lying in prison bound at his hands and feet; fifth, St George of Lydda riding on a horse, brandishing a weapon, and appearing to the judge; sixth, the judge handing to Nazib his paper of release; and seventh, Nazib entering the church, in which the people are gathered, and handing his paper of release to the priest.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The stone-footed man from France with three episodes/events, showing: first, the bishop sitting on a throne before the people, who are gathered in a church, and amongst whom is the man with the petrified foot; second, the man with the petrified foot standing and praying before an icon of the Virgin and Child while his foot is healed; third, the man showing his healed foot to the bishop with the people standing in the background.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Bishop Mercurius, whom the patriarch wanted to remove because of leprosy with seven episodes, showing: first, the leprous bishop Marqorewos standing before Patriarch Zakārǝyās and showing him his leprosy; second, the patriarch warning Marqorewos not to deliver liturgical service; third, Marqorewos praying before the icon of the Virgin and Child; fourth, Virgin and Child healing the leprous bishop and performing gesture of blessing; fifth, the bishop showing himself healed to patriarch Zakārǝyās; sixth, the bishop showing himself healed to his student; and seventh, the healed bishop distributing the Eucharist.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The woman with a broken foot from Hartarom with four episodes, showing: first, two men carrying the woman with a broken foot on a stretcher; second, the woman lying below the icon of the Virgin and Child; third, the broken foot woman again lying below the icon of Virgin and Child on another episode; fourth, the custodian looking the broken woman who is standing near to him with the icon of Virgin and Child depicted from the other side.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel, who became pregnantwith seven episodes, showing: first, Sofǝyā lying on top of a building before the icon of the Virgin and Child and St Mary ordering an to take the child from Sofǝyā's womb; second, an carries away the child from Sofǝyā's womb; third, the giving the child to Finkǝs to adopt him; fourth, the bishop sitting on a throne in front of a group of women accusing Sofǝyā; fifth, the bishop and Sofǝyā sitting next to each other discussing about the manner of the pregnancy; sixth, the bishop receiving the child from the hands of Finkǝs, sitting near to the bishop; and seventh, Sofǝyā's Child, who later become successor of the former bishop (died some time before), surrounded by three persons.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Bārok of Finǝqi whom his enemies could not kill until he had confessed himself to a priest with seven episodes, showing: first, Bārok giving wine in a vessel to his servant to distribute it to the guests; second, his guests sitting and drinking wine; third, the gusts eating food in Bārok's house; fourth; Bārok's enemies attacking him holding several weapons; fifth, a depiction of the icon of the Virgin and Child; sixth, Bārok being consecrated as a monk; seventh, the funeral of Bārok.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The deacon ʾAnǝsṭāsyos who recited the prayer tafaśśǝḥi and was healed by Mary with two episodes, showing: first, ʾAnǝsṭāsyos standing and praying in a church where the icon of the Virgin and Child depicted in front of him; second, ʾAnǝsṭāsyos lying before the icon of Virgin and Child, who is extending her hand towards him and announcing him good news concerning his illness.

A miniature of the art theme No item: AAT1135MMSalusi with four episodes, showing: first, the monk Sālusi surrounded by other monks, who are attacking him; second, the monks preventing him to eat; third, the monk Sālusi standing and praying in a church before the icon of the Virgin and Child and fourth, the monk Sālusi stood up near to the Church after penetrating the wall of the Church with his skull-cap fallen to the ground.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The cannibal of Qǝmǝr with seven episodes, showing: first, the cannibal surrounded by the bodies of those he killed; ; second, peasants and other inhabitants fleeing from the cannibal, with several houses depicted in the background; third, the cannibal offering water to a beggar covered by sores of leprosy; fourth, Jesus Christ, sitting on a throne ordering the demons to add the soul of the cannibal to the hell and the demons holding the soul of the cannibal; fifth; two weighing the cannibal's good and bad deeds in a scale; sixth, St Mary requesting her beloved Son Jesus Christ to give mercy for the soul of the cannibal, and seventh, St Mary holding the soul of the cannibal.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The son of a widow, who became a thief, is saved by Mary with four episodes, showing: first, the widow's son stealing cloth from a church; second, the thief being hanged by the people; third, St Mary ordering the punisher to let him free, and fourth, the thief standing in front of the icon of the St Mary who is depicted on a throne.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The pregnant woman who was caught by the tide while going to the church of St Michael with three episodes, showing: first, the woman holding her new-born child protected by St Mary from the wave; second a church amongst the waves from which the people are fleeing, and third, the woman with her child surrounded by the marvelling people.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Mary gives a thirsty dog water to drink from her shoe with three episodes, showing: first, the thirsty dog searching for water and being kicked by one of the two women sitting near to the well surrounded by plants; second, St Mary giving the dog water to drink from her shoe and, third, the group of virgins who chase out the dog gathered around the well.

A miniature of the art theme Entry into Jerusalem showing: the triumphal entry of Jesus Christ (mounted on a donkey during Palm Sunday) into the city of Jerusalem with Twelve Apostles on a welcoming crowd.
A miniature of the art theme Washing of the Feet with one episode, showing: Jesus Christ washing Peter's feet.
A miniature of the The Last Supper with one episode, showing: the Lord Jesus Christ surrounded by the Twelve Apostles presenting the Eucharist in the form of a lamb.

A miniature of the art theme Agony in the garden with one episode showing: Jesus Christin the Garden of Gethsemane and on the top margin an carrying a cross and giving it to Jesus Christ and Peter, James son of Zebedee and St John the Baptist, all depicted in the form of sadness.

A miniature of the art theme The Striking of the Head, ኵርዓተ፡ ርእሱ፡ with one episode, showing: Jesus Christ crowned with thorns and being stroked at His head by two Jews who hold hammers from His right and left directions.

A miniature of the art theme Crucifixion of Jesus with two episodes from bottom to top, showing: first, St Mary, John and Joseph who are in the act of sadness and are preceded by Jesus Christ who is carrying wood and second, the Jews crucifying Jesus Christ on a cross with two angels collecting Jesus Christ's blood dropping from His body.
A miniature of the art theme Entombment of Jesus showing Joseph and Nicodemus burying Jesus Christ with St Mary and other relatives depicted in the act of sad.
A miniature of the art theme Descent into Hell with one episode showing Jesus Christ saving Adam and Eve and both from the bottom holding His garment from right and left directions.
A miniature of the art theme Ascension of Jesus with one episode showing Jesus Christ in the act of ascension and Twelve Apostles and St Mary depicted from two directions.
A miniature of the art theme Martyrdom with one episode showing St George of Lydda being sliced and beheaded by his opponents.
A miniature of the art theme No item: AT1142Pentecost with one episode showing tongues of fire appearing above Saint St Mary and the Twelve Apostles.
A miniature of the art theme Equestrian Saint showing St George of Lydda (though not named in the captions) who is mounted on a white horse, attacking a dragon to save a girl named Birutāwit.
A miniature of the art theme Equestrian Saint showing the martyr Basilides mounting on a white horse.

A miniature of the art theme Last Judgment with one episode, showing, the Second Coming of Jesus Christ who is depicted in the center with an image of sinners on the left side and an image of those to be saved at the right. On the top margin the scene is surrounded by angels and on the bottom side the demonized devil is depicted with dark color.

A miniature of the art theme Martyrdom with multiple episode of sorrow depictions in relation to the martyr St George of Lydda, first showing a King ordering his soldiers to add St George of Lydda into slaughtering and heating where some techniques and tools of assassination are used in one step after the other; second, the opponents arresting St George of Lydda on a tree; third, they add him alive into a fired metal bed; fourth, they add his powdered flesh to the wind; fourth, the Almighty God giving him the strength and St George of Lydda standing near his God; fifth, the opponents on another part removing his teeth with pincer; sixth, they add him alive into a metal dish of sharp components; and seventh, they heat his body over a heating tool; eighth, they separate his body with other sharp tool; and ninth, they roped his mouth and beheaded him.
A miniature of the art theme Martyrdom showing a group of Jews stoning Saint St Stephen the Protomartyr the martyr to death.
A miniature showing Saint St Mary and Julitta the mother of Cyricus and the child Cyricus in particular stands near to a fire in the depiction.
A miniature showing the youngsters ʾĀnānǝya, ʾĀzarǝya and Misāʾel who are dropped to a fire by enemies rescued by an .

A miniature of the art theme Holy Men Potrait with two episodes, showing: first to the left side, Saint Gabra Manfas Qǝddus standing with his two lions sitting near to him and second, Saint Takla Hāymānot and Saint ʾEwosṭātewos portrayed to the right side of the depiction.
A miniature showing Saint Sǝbäsṭyanos who is roped on a wood and being pierced by two men from his left and right side.

item.t:desc: The name Kirubel, indicated as the owner of the manuscript his wife Kola Gannat as well as his father Nabiyya Lǝ ͗ul and his mother Walatta Ṣadǝqan have been added later in red ink in spaces left empty in the supplications at the beginning and end of the explicit and incipits.
Short Amharic note concerning land distribution and ownership .
This short note address that the manuscript is prepared for Kirubel and his wife Kola Gannat and strongly condemns on those who try to steal the manuscript in the name of Peter and Paul.
item.q: ለዝንቱ፡ መጽሓፍ፡ ዘ፡፡ኪሩቤል ወዘምስለ ፡ ኮለ ገነት ዘፈሐቆ፡ ወዘሰረቆ፡ ወዘተአገሎ፡ በስልጣነ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ወጳውሎስ፡ ውጉዘ፡ ከመ፡ አርዮስ ፡ለይኩን።

item.t:desc: Cotton that are tied directly to the tops of the pages are used to protect each miniatures.

binding.decoNote: Blind-tooled leather cover, with textile inlays
Parchment guards are placed around each quire.

history.origin: The manuscript is dated to by the Art Institute of Chicago, but nothing is indicated concerning the method of their datation. The manuscript can be dated to , an inclusive period of categorization of all manuscripts.
history.provenance: Nothing is indicated in the manuscript concerning its specific Ethiopian place of origin in the text, but it is indicated in the website of the current holders of the manuscript that the manuscript was sold to the Art Institute of Chicago in 2002 by the institute, Sam Fogg Rare Books and Manuscripts, London.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
